commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4bf1467e6e44e63a45b25f03f6763cb882568522)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `richMode` option to `markdownToTelegramHtml`, converts generated blockquote and heading tags to `` on the default path, and adds `
` to the legacy Telegram HTML allowlist. PR surface: Source +13. Total +13 across 1 file. Reproducibility: yes. from source and the linked user report: current non-rich Telegram reply rendering can produce `
` and sends it through `parse_mode: HTML`, while the linked issue includes real Telegram Web unsupported-message evidence. I did not run live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer code changes plus real Telegram proof before merge; the repair lane should not take over while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(telegram): avoid Bot API 10.1 HTML tags in non-rich messages
target.body:
## Description Telegram Web clients show "This message is not supported" when receiving messages containing Bot API 10.1+ HTML features like `
` even when sent via regular `sendMessage` with `parse_mode: HTML`. OpenClaw's `markdownToTelegramHtml` was emitting these tags unconditionally via `renderTelegramHtml`. ## Fix - Added a `richMode` option to `markdownToTelegramHtml` - In non-rich mode (default), `
` and `

`-`

` tags are rendered as `` instead of their Bot API 10.1+ counterparts - The rich rendering path (`markdownToTelegramRichHtml`) is unchanged - Also added `
` to `TELEGRAM_SIMPLE_HTML_TAGS` since it's a widely supported basic tag ## Fixes Fixes #93794 Co-Authored-By: Claude
target.author: xydttsw

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
